overview: TLA is a synergy-driven set where the four nations use elemental bending and Allies. Success in draft requires balancing the Learn/Lesson system with specific nation synergies.

mechanics.name: Airbending
mechanics.description: Exiles a permanent; owner can pay {2} to return it. Used for tempo or saving your own units.
mechanics.draft_tip: Prioritize flyers in WU to capitalize on the tempo gain from Airbending.

mechanics.name: Waterbend {X}
mechanics.description: Tap creatures/artifacts to reduce generic costs. Rewards defensive boards and token generation.
mechanics.draft_tip: Clue tokens are excellent fuel for Waterbend spells in UB and UR.

mechanics.name: Earthbending X
mechanics.description: Turns a land into a creature with X +1/+1 counters and haste. Late-game mana sink.
mechanics.draft_tip: Ensure you have enough defensive commons in Green to survive until Earthbending becomes relevant.

mechanics.name: Firebending
mechanics.description: Provides mana or triggers when attacking or casting multiple spells per turn.
mechanics.draft_tip: Draft low-curve red decks to ensure you can double-spell frequently.

mechanics.name: Allies
mechanics.description: Tribal scaling that rewards having multiple Ally creatures on the battlefield.
mechanics.draft_tip: GW and RW are the core Ally colors. Don't let high-impact Allies pass.

archetypes.name: Air Nomads / Flyers
archetypes.description: Winning through the air with evasive creatures and Airbending tempo.
archetypes.strategy: Lock down the ground and use Glider Flight and Appa to win the race.

archetypes.name: Water Tribe / Draw Two
archetypes.description: Control deck utilizing Clues and 'Second Card' triggers.
archetypes.strategy: Grind with Northern Tribe Scout and use Waterbend to cast expensive control spells.

archetypes.name: Fire Nation / Aggro
archetypes.description: High-velocity attacks and direct burn damage.
archetypes.strategy: Use Fire Nation Soldier and Agni Kai to keep the pressure high early.

archetypes.name: Earth Kingdom / Midrange
archetypes.description: Big creatures and mana ramp using Earthbending for reach.
archetypes.strategy: Survive the early game and finish with animated Earthbending lands.

archetypes.name: Team Avatar / Allies
archetypes.description: The strongest tribal archetype focusing on go-wide scaling.
archetypes.strategy: Curve out with Allies and use The Earth King or United Front as finishers.

general_tips: White is the deepest color; if it's open, your Ally synergies will be top-tier.
Lessons are vital for Bo1 play; ensure you have 2-3 utility Lessons in your sideboard.
Firebending requires you to be proactive; don't draft too many reactive spells in BR or UR.
